mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that outweigh corporate earnings resilience, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer prices indicate robust consumer demand outpacing supply constraints, which forces equity markets to price in higher corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and CPIAUCSL fail to produce a positive mean forward return over 60 days in walk-forward tests, or Rank IC drops below zero.

mechanism_narrative: During active monetary tightening cycles, positive month-over-month growth in the industrial production index signals resilient manufacturing output that neutralizes rate hike headwinds, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during tightening periods, with a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising household income, which directly supports corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll revisions consistently coincide with negative 20-day forward returns, yielding a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates below 1.5% on a quarterly basis, aggregate demand contraction triggers earnings downgrades across cyclical sectors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.5%, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising federal funds rates and accelerating consumer price indices creates a stagflationary environment that compresses equity multiples and reduces risk appetite, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative returns, with a positive mean forward return and Rank IC above 0.04 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, accelerating housing starts indicate renewed consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which flows through to equity markets and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases during recovery phases consistently precede negative 5-day returns, or the signal predictive power vanishes when tested against a buy-and-hold benchmark.
